food_subgroup: Fast food combo meal — spicy fried chicken burger with cheese, large fries, and large soft drink

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for McSpicy with Cheese Upsized Meal. Closest defensible anchor is FOODON_00002383 (chicken sandwich food product). The full meal includes fries and beverage which are not captured by this single code.
ontology.foodex2_description: Chicken burger — fast food composite meal
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP used as closest defensible category for a fast-food chicken burger composite meal. No exact FoodEx2 entry exists for the McSpicy with Cheese Upsized Meal as a complete combo.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No applicable SNOMED CT food concept identified for this fast-food combo meal product.

preparation_method: served with fries and soft drink

serving.description: 1 upsized meal (McSpicy with Cheese burger + large fries + large soft drink)
serving.natural_language_portion: One upsized meal consisting of one McSpicy with Cheese burger (approximately 220 g), one large portion of fries (approximately 150 g), and one large soft drink (approximately 380 ml / 380 g), totalling approximately 750 g.

nutrient_source.title: McDonald's Singapore Nutrition Information — McSpicy with Cheese, Large Fries, Large Coca-Cola
nutrient_source.organization: McDonald's Singapore (via USDA Branded Food and McDonald's published nutrition data cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central composite estimation)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: McDonald's Singapore publishes per-item nutrition data. The upsized meal is a composite of: McSpicy with Cheese burger (~220 g, ~490 kcal), large fries (~150 g, ~490 kcal), and large Coca-Cola (~380 ml, ~150 kcal). Per-100g values are derived by dividing total meal nutrients by total meal weight (~750 g). Core macros cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central entries for analogous fast-food spicy chicken sandwich with cheese (USDA FDC ID 2103726), fast-food french fries (USDA FDC ID 2103689), and cola beverage (USDA FDC ID 174851). Values represent a weighted composite estimate.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — Atkinson FS et al.; and University of Sydney GI Database analogous entries for fast-food composite meals
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the McSpicy with Cheese Upsized Meal as a composite. GI estimated at 65 (medium) based on weighted analogous components: white burger bun GI ~70 (University of Sydney GI Database), fast-food french fries GI ~63 (University of Sydney GI Database), cola beverage GI ~63 (University of Sydney GI Database). Weighted average across carbohydrate contribution of each component yields approximately 65. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (65 × 141.75) / 100 ≈ 92 for full meal; however, per-serving GL reported here uses the standard 750 g serving with 141.75 g total carbohydrate: GL = 65 × 141.75 / 100 = 92.1. Note: GL per serving is reported as 26.0 using a reference 200 g portion for per-serving GL standardisation consistent with Veritas template convention. Full meal GL is approximately 92.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content — approximately 2175 mg sodium per full upsized meal (290 mg per 100 g × 750 g), exceeding 94% of the WHO recommended daily limit of 2300 mg
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit consumption of this meal due to its very high sodium load from the seasoned fried chicken, processed cheese, and salted fries, which can acutely elevate blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load from refined carbohydrates in burger bun, large fries, and cola beverage, combined with high total caloric density
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this meal or consume it only occasionally in strictly reduced portions, as the high glycemic load (estimated full-meal GL ~92) and high refined carbohydrate content will cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation; substituting the cola with water and reducing fries portion size would substantially lower glycemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Combination of high saturated fat, trans fat, sodium, and total caloric density in a single meal
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should treat this meal as an occasional indulgence only, as its saturated fat content (~13.5 g per meal), trans fat (~0.75 g per meal), and very high sodium (~2175 mg per meal) collectively contribute to atherogenic risk, endothelial dysfunction, and elevated blood pressure.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The McSpicy with Cheese Upsized Meal is a McDonald's Singapore fast-food combo meal consisting of three components: the McSpicy with Cheese burger, a large portion of french fries, and a large soft drink (typically Coca-Cola). The McSpicy burger features a deep-fried, spicy-seasoned chicken thigh fillet in a crispy breaded coating, served in a toasted sesame-seed bun with shredded lettuce, mayonnaise, and a slice of processed American-style cheese. The chicken patty is marinated with a proprietary spice blend that delivers a distinctively fiery heat, making it one of McDonald's Singapore's most iconic and enduring menu items. The upsized (large) format increases the fries and beverage portions compared to the standard meal. The full meal weighs approximately 750 g and delivers roughly 1140 kcal, making it a high-calorie, energy-dense fast-food option. Nutritionally, the meal is high in refined carbohydrates (from the bun, fries, and cola), moderate-to-high in fat (including saturated fat from the cheese and frying oil), and very high in sodium. The glycemic load of the full meal is estimated at approximately 92, driven primarily by the large fries and cola. Clinically, this meal is of high concern for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease due to its sodium, saturated fat, trans fat, and high glycemic load. The meal is certified Halal by McDonald's Singapore and is widely available across McDonald's outlets in Singapore. It is a popular choice for lunch and dinner among Singaporeans and tourists seeking a satisfying, spicy fast-food experience.

confidence_reason: McDonald's Singapore publishes per-item nutrition data on its official website, and per-100g values for this composite meal are derived by weighted estimation across the three meal components (burger, fries, cola) using McDonald's Singapore published data cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central analogous entries. No single approved primary source entry exists for this exact composite meal, hence confidence is medium rather than high. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database component data.
notes: 1. This record represents the full McSpicy with Cheese Upsized Meal as a composite food item (burger + large fries + large cola, ~750 g total). Per-100g values are weighted estimates. 2. The food_group has been corrected from the input value 'Dairy and eggs' to 'Mixed', as this is a composite fast-food meal containing chicken, bread, potato, and dairy components. 3. Vitamin B1, B2, B3, B6, B12, folate, vitamin D, vitamin E, phosphorus, magnesium, zinc, and selenium are set to -1 as these micronutrients are not individually reported in McDonald's Singapore published nutrition data for this meal, and no approved primary source provides these values for this exact composite. 4. GI is estimated at 65 (medium) based on weighted analogous components from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement exists for this composite meal. 5. Glycemic load per serving of 26.0 is calculated using a standardised 200 g reference portion per Veritas template convention; full-meal GL is approximately 92. 6. The McSpicy with Cheese burger is a Singapore-specific McDonald's product and is not available in all markets. 7. Halal certification is maintained by McDonald's Singapore under MUIS. 8. Price per field survey listing: SGD 12.40 (fixed, both outlets).
